title: UN Security Council Emergency Session on Iran
detail: The UN Security Council convenes an emergency session on Iran following a request from the US, UK, and France. The session features testimony from human rights investigators and diaspora witnesses. Russia and China oppose any binding resolution but agree to a presidential statement calling for 'restraint.' The US introduces a draft resolution demanding international access to investigate the massacres.

title: Death Toll Estimates Sharply Diverge
detail: A grim picture emerges as different sources report vastly different casualty figures. Iran's government maintains 127 total deaths. HRANA estimates over 4,200. Time magazine, citing leaked military communications, reports a range of 30,000-36,500 killed during January 8-9 alone. The massive discrepancy reflects the information blackout and regime efforts to conceal evidence.

title: Iran Expels All Remaining Foreign Journalists
detail: Iran's Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance revokes the press credentials of all remaining foreign correspondents, giving them 48 hours to leave the country. Reporters from Reuters, AFP, and AP are escorted to the airport. The move further isolates Iran from independent verification of conditions on the ground.
